task: I’m trying to put together a simple record of a recurring issue so I can show what’s happening instead of just describing it from memory. What’s the easiest way to capture the important details on my phone and keep them organized so I can compare them later?

turns.agent_response:
The easiest approach is to use a simple **repeatable log** on your phone—either in **Notes**, **Google Keep**, **Apple Notes**, or a **spreadsheet** if you want easier comparison later.

### Simple setup
Create one entry per occurrence with the same fields each time:

- **Date / time**
- **What happened**
- **Where you were / what you were doing**
- **How long it lasted**
- **Severity** (1–10)
- **Any trigger or pattern you noticed**
- **Photo / screenshot / voice note** if relevant

### Easiest format
If you want the fastest method, use a note template like this:

**Issue Log**
- Date:
- Time:
- Event:
- Symptoms/details:
- Trigger:
- Duration:
- Severity:
- Evidence:

Then copy/paste it each time.

### Best way to stay organized
- Use **one note per issue type** or **one spreadsheet with rows for each event**
- Add a **title with the date** so it’s easy to search later
- Keep **photos/screenshots attached right under the entry**
- If you can, use **bullet points** instead of paragraphs

### If you want to compare later
A spreadsheet is best because you can sort by:
- date
- severity
- duration
- trigger

If you want, I can make you a **very short phone-friendly template** you can copy into Notes right now.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, basically that. But I’d want it a little tighter and less fluffy.

I’d do this on the phone:

- one note or one sheet per issue
- same fields every time
- no paragraphs
- attach photo / screenshot right there

I’d want the template to force the useful stuff, like:

- date/time
- location
- exact symptom
- what changed right before it
- duration
- severity
- what evidence I have
- what I checked already

That last part matters. Otherwise you end up with a story, not a record.

If you can, make it sortable later. Spreadsheet is better if this is something recurring and you actually want to compare patterns instead of just collecting clutter.

Give me the short phone template. Something I can paste into Notes and use without thinking.

turns.agent_response:
Here’s a tight, phone-friendly template you can paste into Notes:

**ISSUE LOG**
- Date/time:
- Location:
- Exact symptom:
- What changed right before:
- Duration:
- Severity (1–10):
- Evidence: (photo/screenshot/link)
- What I checked already:

**Optional if useful**
- Trigger/pattern:
- Result/outcome:
- Follow-up needed:

If you want it even faster, use this shorter version:

**ISSUE LOG**
- Date/time:
- Location:
- Symptom:
- Before it started:
- Duration:
- Severity:
- Evidence:
- Checked:

If you want, I can also turn this into a **single-line spreadsheet version** with column headers.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Short enough to actually use.

The shorter version is the one I’d keep:

ISSUE LOG
- Date/time:
- Location:
- Symptom:
- Before it started:
- Duration:
- Severity:
- Evidence:
- Checked:

That “Checked” line is the important part. Keeps it from turning into a sloppy narrative.

Single-line spreadsheet headers would be useful too, if you’ve got that.
